planning_principles: Working RC editor is benchmark for coverage/usability, not canonical truth.
Source precedence for semantics: official docs > RC0/XML > repo schema/frontmatter > style refs.
No monolithic rewrite; incremental planner-capability gains only.

ROUTING.current_state: Validated structure exists in research artifacts, but no explicit editable routing-matrix state model is present in app-facing schema/state.
ROUTING.target_state: Per-memory routing model with explicit Track and Input/Rhythm matrices, destination groups, and conditional phones behavior represented in typed state.
ROUTING.smallest_corrective_step: Introduce MemoryRouting schema + state adapter with two sub-maps (routing_track, routing_input_rhythm) and destination keys MAIN/SUB1/SUB2/PHONES.

schema: enum definitions for destinations and members
ui: then editable toggles with validation hints

ROUTING.validation_requirement: Fixture-based checks: route matrix round-trip (state -> serialized representation -> state) + destination/member completeness checks against validated claim set.

TRACK_BOUNCE_IN.current_state: Bounce In semantics are validated but currently represented as evidence/claims, not typed per-track planning state.
TRACK_BOUNCE_IN.target_state: Each track has a typed input-source set and bounce intent that can be planned, validated, and diffed across memories.
TRACK_BOUNCE_IN.smallest_corrective_step: Add TrackInputSourceSet type and attach to each TrackPlan object (mic1/mic2/inst1L/inst1R/inst2L/inst2R/rhythm + bounce_enabled).
TRACK_BOUNCE_IN.validation_requirement: Track fixture tests for source-set persistence and conflict warnings (e.g., bounce enabled with empty source set).

ASSIGN.current_state: ASSIGN1..16 field family is validated, but no enforceable typed assign-bank model is operationalized.
ASSIGN.target_state: Assign bank represented as 16 typed slots with required fields, light invariants, and deterministic diff output.
ASSIGN.smallest_corrective_step: Implement AssignSlot schema (x16) with switch, source, mode, act_low, act_high, target, target_low, target_high plus invariant validators.
ASSIGN.validation_requirement: Fixture suite proving all 16 slots serialize/deserialize and pass required-field + range checks.

CTL_FUNC.current_state: Pedal modes and ext/exp structures are validated but flattened; no dedicated control-plan model is exposed.
CTL_FUNC.target_state: Mode-indexed control plan with separate pedal-mode map, external controls, expression controls, and isolated compatibility metadata.
CTL_FUNC.smallest_corrective_step: Create ControlPlan schema: pedal_modes[1..3][pedal1..9], ext_ctrl[ctl1..4], expression[exp1..2], compatibility_metadata.
CTL_FUNC.validation_requirement: Mode coverage fixtures (all 3 modes and 9 pedals each) + ext/exp min/max persistence checks.

MEMORY_SET_PLANNING_UX.current_state: Current outputs are research/audit artifacts; no planner-first memory aggregate workflow is visible.
MEMORY_SET_PLANNING_UX.target_state: Planner-first MemoryPlan workflow: draft memory, validate, diff vs baseline, assemble setlist, mark export/sync readiness.
MEMORY_SET_PLANNING_UX.smallest_corrective_step: Introduce MemoryPlan aggregate object + minimal planner workflow (draft -> validate -> diff summary).
MEMORY_SET_PLANNING_UX.validation_requirement: End-to-end fixture scenario with 3-5 memory plans showing meaningful diffs and validation gating.

recommended_implementation_order: 2) TRACK Bounce In / input-source typed model
6) Incremental domain UI editors on top of validated state

stop_doing: Stop treating evidence artifacts as a substitute for runtime state models.
Stop flattening domain semantics into generic key/value blocks without typed constraints.
Stop mixing compatibility/guardrail metadata directly into semantic planner fields.
keep_doing: Keep explicit source-tier provenance for every semantic decision.
Keep fixture-driven validation before widening UI scope.
Keep benchmark comparison against working editor for usability/coverage checks.
start_doing: Start implementing typed domain schemas as first-class planner state.
Start adding deterministic diff outputs for memory-level changes.
Start shipping vertical slices (state + validation + minimal UI) per domain.

milestones.name: Structural convergence
exit_criteria: All four domain schemas implemented and wired into MemoryPlan draft state.
No domain remains claim-only in planner core models.

milestones.name: Validation fixtures and diff confidence
scope: state diff renderer for memory plans
exit_criteria: Fixture suite passes for routing/track/assign/ctl plan objects.
Memory-level diff output is deterministic and human-reviewable.

milestones.name: Planner-first UX
scope: domain editors layered over validated state
exit_criteria: User can build/edit a small set of memories with validation feedback.
Planner UX supports comparison and confidence checks before export/sync work.

risks.biggest_semantic_risk: Overfitting to benchmark editor-specific metadata (or app-bundle quirks) where official-doc/RC0 semantics are silent.
risks.biggest_ux_risk: Adding domain depth without progressive disclosure, causing planners to feel dense and error-prone.
risks.biggest_architecture_risk: UI-first patching before typed state convergence, leading to fragile coupling and rework during sync/export integration.
